questions.text: What shape are warning signs on UK roads?
questions.explanation: Warning signs are triangular with a red border and yellow background to alert drivers of potential hazards.

questions.text: What colour background do information signs have?
questions.explanation: Information signs have a blue background and are rectangular, providing directions and information about services.

questions.text: What is the national speed limit for cars on a motorway in the UK?
questions.explanation: The maximum speed limit for cars on motorways is 70 mph (112 km/h), though you must drive at a safe speed for conditions.

questions.text: Which lane should you primarily use on a motorway?
questions.explanation: You should keep to the left lane and only use the middle and right lanes for overtaking.

options: No, they must hold a full license
questions.explanation: Only drivers who hold a full, valid driving license can use motorways. Learners must not drive on motorways.

questions.text: What should you do if you see a warning sign for children crossing?
options: Slow down and be prepared to stop
Increase your speed to cross quickly
questions.explanation: When you see a warning sign for children crossing, you must slow down and be prepared to stop, as children may suddenly cross the road.

questions.text: What does the knife and fork symbol on a blue sign indicate?
questions.explanation: The knife and fork symbol on an information (blue) sign indicates a restaurant or food service ahead.

questions.text: When overtaking on a motorway, which lane should you move to?
questions.explanation: You can move to any lane to the right for overtaking, but you must return to the left lane once you've completed the manoeuvre.

questions.text: What is the speed limit for HGVs on UK motorways?
questions.explanation: Heavy goods vehicles (HGVs) have a maximum speed limit of 60 mph on motorways, lower than cars.

questions.text: Can you stop on a motorway hard shoulder for a rest break?
options: No, never without a valid reason
questions.explanation: The hard shoulder is for emergency use only. If you feel fatigued, you should exit at the next service station.
